judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 matches all four expectations explicitly (citing the migration file, endorsing same-commit doc fixes, and recommending dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at source), while Report 1 hits only the first two and gives weaker, less explicit answers on (c) and (d).

report_1.evidence: New provider calls (e.g. a `refund(...)`) should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`. ... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — `charge_invoice` builds `chargekit.Client(...)` ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` — `run_dunning_pass` builds its own client ... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` also imports from `chargekit`, but only the exception classes — this is the explicitly sanctioned boundary exception ... not a violation.

report_2.evidence: New provider calls (e.g. a `refund(...)`) belong in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` — imports only `chargekit.errors` exception types; the doc explicitly sanctions this as the one exception. Two are violations ... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13`

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-28` has **6** fields — it adds `external_ref`. So the doc's "exactly" is wrong; `external_ref` is missing. (This field is real and used: `invoices.py:17` sets it from the charge result.)

report_2.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`models/invoice.py:16-28`) also includes `external_ref`, which the charge route populates (`invoices.py:17`) and which was added by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`.

report_1.evidence: so I'd flag/fix them now (before writing refund code), not bundle them silently into the feature.

report_2.evidence: Per the contract-as-law principle, doc/code drift is a bug, not a someday-cleanup. The document should be corrected to match reality (or the code brought back to match the document) in the **same commit** as the change that touches these surfaces — i.e., as part of the refund work, since that work touches the payment boundary directly.

report_1.evidence: The doc undercounts and omits `payment_pending`. I'd update the claim to list all 5 types (or, if `payment_pending` handling is unwanted, remove that branch from the code) rather than leave the stated count wrong.

report_2.evidence: rather than hard-coding a count that silently goes stale (project documentation's "don't cite a number, read the source" point), I'd drop the "4" and either list the handled types as they appear in `webhooks.py` or point at `webhooks.py` as the source of truth.
